geopolitics_summary: EU-US trade tensions over forced labor tariffs and China sanctions threaten transatlantic stability; Middle East energy crisis drives green investment exemptions; ETS review looming July.

themes.headline: EU households to cut peak demand as industry and AI power soars
themes.why_it_matters: Peak shaving via AI-driven demand-side response reduces residual-load volatility, compressing peak-to-off-peak DA spreads and month-ahead Cal curves. Critical for grid stability as AI data centers strain capacity.

themes.headline: EU ETS review scheduled July amid Poland criticism of carbon policy
themes.why_it_matters: July Commission review of Emissions Trading System creates near-term regulatory uncertainty on EUA floor prices, free allocation, and carbon leakage rules. High desk relevance for Q3 EUA curve positioning.

themes.headline: Commission exempts green energy investments from EU fiscal spending rules
themes.why_it_matters: Fiscal flexibility accelerates renewable capex deployment and grid upgrades, increasing near-term supply-side capacity. Downward pressure on power prices via faster wind/solar ramp-up, especially weeks-months horizon.

themes.headline: Russian shadow fleet relies on EU-linked insurers despite sanctions
themes.why_it_matters: Indirect: EU crackdown on shadow-fleet insurance may tighten Russian oil sanctions, reducing crude supply globally. Lower oil inventories and higher Brent can lift EU gas via crude-linked LNG contract indexation, particularly for spot TTF.

themes.headline: US natural gas pipeline capacity surges 44.9 Bcf/d in 2026–27, mostly Texas
themes.why_it_matters: Indirect: US Gulf Coast LNG export infrastructure expansion lowers Henry Hub-TTF spread, making US LNG cheaper for NW Europe. Supports summer 2026 storage refill and moderates TTF upside into winter.

watchlist: EU trade deal final plenary vote June 16; monitor tariff outcome risk to transatlantic LNG flows.
Commission ETS review expected July; watch EUA floor price and free allocation policy changes.
El Niño heat outlook — hydro risk for Alpine/Scandinavian power generation in H2 2026.
China-Russia sanctions escalation; indirect energy commodity impact via geopolitical oil/LNG volatility.
